response:
# E-Commerce Product Listing (Grid, Sort, Filter, Add-to-Cart) Accessibility Design Plan > **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after implementation. > **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA > **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver), keyboard-only users, low-vision users, users who rely on text-not-color, users with motion sensitivity > **Assistive technologies:** NVDA (Windows + Firefox/Chrome), JAWS (Windows + Chrome), VoiceOver (macOS + Safari), keyboard-only (no AT) **Feature:** An e-commerce product listing page that renders 24 product cards per page (847 total) with a sort dropdown, a collapsible filter panel (category/brand/price-range-slider/color), per-card Add-to-Cart with confirmation feedback, a header cart count, and prev/next + page-selector pagination. **Risk Level:** Medium (breadth-driven — many small sub-patterns, two non-obvious decisions: star rating representation and sale-price announcement; one High-risk sub-component: the two-handle range slider) **Component Type:** Composite page — List, Native Select, Disclosure + Checkbox Group, Dual-thumb Slider, Live Region (status), Navigation landmark --- ## Scope & Context **What is being built?** Accessibility design for an e-commerce product grid page with sort, filter, per-card add-to-cart, and pagination. **What user need does it address?** Shoppers must be able to browse, sort, filter, evaluate (rating, price, sale, availability), and add products to a cart — and receive confirmation that the add succeeded — regardless of how they perceive or operate the page. **Who needs accessibility?** - **Screen reader users** — need product semantics (list, count, headings), spoken star ratings, spoken sale-price context, slider value announcements, and a cart-add confirmation they cannot see. - **Keyboard-only users** — need to reach and operate every control (sort, filter toggles, checkboxes, both slider thumbs, add-to-cart, pagination) in a logical order with a visible focus indicator. - **Low-vision users** — need 200%/400% zoom reflow, sufficient contrast on the "Sale" badge, availability badges, and focus rings. - **Users who rely on text-not-color** — availability ("Low Stock") and sale state must not be conveyed by color alone. - **Motion-sensitive users** — the toast and "Added ✓" transition must respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA. **Assistive technologies to support:** NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, keyboard-only. Live-region and slider behavior must be verified across all three screen readers because `aria-live` timing and slider `aria-valuetext` support vary between them. **Risk level rationale:** Most sub-patterns are individually Low/Medium. The page is Medium overall because of breadth (10+ sub-patterns must all be correct) and two design traps: 1. **Star rating** — Unicode glyphs are read character-by-character or as decorative noise by some AT. 2. **Sale price** — naive ``/`` is announced as "deleted"/"struck through" with no "was/now" context. One sub-component, the **dual-thumb price slider**, is High-risk (third-party component with no ARIA, coordinated min/max thumbs, keyboard model). **Existing code this modifies (from fixture context):** - Product cards are `
` with click handlers → must become `
    `/`
  • ` with a real heading + real `` link. (WCAG 1.3.1, 2.1.1, 4.1.2) - Star ratings are Unicode `★★★☆☆` with no SR equivalent → replace with `aria-hidden` glyphs + `sr-only` text. (WCAG 1.1.1, 1.3.1) - Sale prices use visual strikethrough only → add SR-only "Was … now …" context. (WCAG 1.3.1) - Add-to-Cart has no post-action announcement → add a pre-existing polite live region. (WCAG 4.1.3) - Cart badge updates silently → update an `aria-label` on the cart link. (WCAG 4.1.2) - Price range slider is custom with no ARIA → add full slider ARIA on two thumbs. (WCAG 4.1.2) **Constraints:** - Product cards MUST be list items, not table rows (semantic + visual requirement). - Star ratings are font/Unicode icons that must be replaced with accessible equivalents. - Price range slider is a third-party component with limited ARIA — we must inject ARIA attributes onto its thumb elements explicitly; we cannot assume the library provides them. - The cart toast renders **outside** the `
    ` landmark (bottom-right overlay). Because it is inserted into the DOM at add-time, it must NOT be the live region itself — a separate, pre-existing live region carries the announcement. --- ## Semantic Structure Plan ### Landmark regions | Landmark | Element | Purpose | WCAG | |----------|---------|---------|------| | Banner | `
    ` | Logo, cart link (with live count), site nav | 1.3.1 | | Main | `
    ` | Page heading, results summary, sort, results grid, pagination | 1.3.1, 2.4.1 | | Complementary / search filters | `
    ` (or `
    ` containing a `
    `) | The filter panel | 1.3.1 | | Navigation (pagination) | `
    ` | Prev/next + page selector | 1.3.1, 2.4.1 | | Live region (status) | `
    ` (visually hidden, persistent) | Cart-add confirmation text | 4.1.3 | Two `
    ` elements exist (site nav in header, pagination in main). Each is distinguished by an accessible name (`aria-label`) per WCAG 1.3.1 so AT users can tell them apart in a landmark list. ### Heading hierarchy ``` h1 Outdoor Gear — Jackets (page H1, one per page) h2 Filter products (filter panel heading; the
    label may duplicate or the H2 serves as its label via aria-labelledby) h3 Category h3 Brand h3 Price range h3 Color h2 847 products (or this is a non-heading results summary — see note) h3 Seaborn Down Jacket (product name = card heading, repeats per card) h3 Granite Peak Fleece h3 … ``` **Card heading-level decision (a non-trivial choice — document it):** The fixture says product name is `

    ` if within a `
    `, or `

    ` if the page has section headings. Because this page has an H1 (page title) and H2 section headings (Filter products, results region), **each product name is an `

    `**. This keeps the outline legible: H1 → H2 (regions) → H3 (products), no skipped levels (WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.6 Headings and Labels, 2.4.10 Section Headings AAA-adjacent best practice). Do not make every product an H2 sibling of the page regions — that flattens the outline and makes "skip to next region" via heading navigation unreliable. The results summary ("847 products" / "Showing 1–24 of 847") is presented as visible text inside `
    `. It does **not** need to be a heading; it is associated with the list as preceding context. (If the team prefers it as a heading for heading-navigation, an `

    ` is acceptable — but then the product cards remain `

    `.) ### Product list structure The grid is a **list of items**, not a data table (explicit constraint). Use: ```
      (CSS grid for visual layout; semantics remain a list)
    • product card
      (optional but recommended — gives each card a self-contained region with an accessible name) image link (decorative-vs-informative decision below)

      Seaborn Down Jacket

      rating (sr-only text + aria-hidden glyphs) price (sale pattern) availability badge (text, not color-only)
      • …
    ``` **Note on `role="list"`:** Authors sometimes add `role="list"` to a `
      ` to defend against `list-style: none` removing list semantics in Safari + VoiceOver. This is a known WebKit quirk. **Plan: keep the native `
        `/`
      • ` and add `role="list"` to the `
          ` only if `list-style: none` is applied** (it will be, for a grid). This is belt-and-suspenders, not a replacement for native semantics. (WCAG 1.3.1) **Product image link target (decorative vs informative):** Each card has the product image inside a link to the same product as the name link. To avoid two adjacent links with the same destination producing redundant/confusing output: - **Preferred:** Make the image `alt=""` (decorative) when the adjacent text link already names the product — the visible product-name link carries the accessible name. This is the fixture's known-pitfall #8 ("`alt=""` when a link provides the accessible name"). The image-wrapping link should then either be removed (let the name link be the sole link) or, if both links must exist for layout/click-area reasons, the image link gets `aria-label="Seaborn Down Jacket"` and the `` gets `alt=""` so the link is named once, not described verbosely. - **Decision for this plan:** Render **one** product link — wrap the `

          ` text in the ``, and make the product image a sibling with `alt=""` inside its own link only if a separate image click-target is a hard product requirement. If both links are kept, image `alt=""` + link `aria-label` = product name. (WCAG 1.1.1, 2.4.4) ### Form structure (filter panel) - The filter panel is a `` inside the `
          `/`
          `. - Each filter group (Category, Brand, Color) is a `
          ` with a `` naming the group. (WCAG 1.3.1, 3.3.2) - Checkboxes are native `` with associated visible `
          ` after `
          ` in DOM and reorder visually — but DOM-after-main risks reading-order mismatch, so prefer an explicit "Skip filters / Skip to results" link at the top of the filter panel). (WCAG 2.4.1, 1.3.2) ### HTML structure stub (semantics + ARIA only — no implementation code) ``` Skip to main content
          (banner)
          …
          cart icon (aria-hidden) + visible badge "3" (aria-hidden)
          (or
          )

          Filter products

          Skip filters, go to results
          Category … checkboxes …
          Brand … checkboxes …
          Price range … dual-thumb slider, two role="slider" elements …
          Color … checkboxes …

          Jackets

          Showing 1–24 of 847 products

          (results summary, visible)
            (role="list" because list-style:none)

          • Seaborn Down Jacket

            ★★★★☆ Rated 4 out of 5 stars

            (sale price pattern) $149 Was $149, now $89 $89 (or one visible span the SR also reads — see slider section) Sale (text, not color-only)

            Low Stock

            (text badge, not color-only)
            • …
          Previous 1 2 … Next
          Seaborn Down Jacket added to cart. View Cart
          ``` --- ## Interaction Pattern Design ### Interactive Elements Table | Widget | APG Pattern | Keyboard | ARIA | WCAG | |--------|-------------|----------|------|------| | Skip link | (Link) — [WCAG technique G1] | Tab → Enter | native `` | 2.4.1 | | Product name link | (Link) [APG Link](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/link/) | Tab → Enter | native `` | 2.1.1, 2.4.4, 4.1.2 | | Sort dropdown | Native ``; current = selected `
          ``` **ARIA:** ` {count} (persistent, first-paint, anywhere in DOM)
          ``` On add: write `"{name} added to cart. Cart now has {count} items."` into `#cart-status`; do not move focus; toast (visual) is `aria-hidden`. **ARIA:** per-button `aria-label` with product name (4.1.2, 2.4.6 — NOT `title`); cart link `aria-label` updated on count change (4.1.2); pre-existing `role="status"` mutated on add (4.1.3); toast container `aria-hidden` (4.1.3); "✓" `aria-hidden`. **Keyboard:** Space/Enter activates; focus remains on button. **Focus management:** no focus move to toast; persistent header cart link is the durable "View Cart" path. **State:** transient "Added ✓" visual (3s) + reduced-motion respected; cart count via `aria-label`; confirmation via status region. **Tests:** **(a)** `role="status"` exists empty on first render; **(b)** on add, region is mutated (not recreated) and SR announces the sentence; **(c)** toast does NOT announce (aria-hidden); **(d)** cart badge increment alone does NOT announce; **(e)** cart link `aria-label` updates; **(f)** each button's accessible name includes its product; **(g)** focus stays on the button; **(h)** reduced-motion disables the transition. **WCAG:** 4.1.2, 4.1.3, 2.4.6, 2.3.3, 2.2.1. **a11y-critic checkpoint:** live region pre-exists and is mutated (NOT created at add-time, NOT on the toast); badge is `aria-label` not per-increment live region; per-button names unique; `title` not used; focus not stranded. ### Task 7: Results status announcements (sort / filter / page / loading) 🔍 **Review checkpoint 7** (may share region/checkpoint with Task 6) **Files:** `components/ResultsStatusRegion.tsx` (or reuse `#cart-status` with care), wiring in `ProductGrid.tsx` **Structure Stub:** ```
            …
          ``` **ARIA:** pre-existing `role="status"` mutated on sort/filter/page/loading (4.1.3); `aria-busy` on the list during fetch (4.1.3). **State:** "Loading results…" → "Showing 1–24 of 847" / "312 products" / "Sorted by …". **Tests:** each event mutates the region; announcements are polite (don't interrupt); two near-simultaneous events queue rather than clobber; `aria-busy` toggles. **WCAG:** 4.1.3. **a11y-critic checkpoint:** single pre-existing region (not per-event injection); polite not assertive; no double-announce with the cart region; loading state exposed. ### Task 8: Price range slider (two thumbs) — High-risk 🔍 **Review checkpoint 8** (deepest checkpoint) **Files:** `components/PriceRangeSlider.tsx` (ARIA wrapper over the third-party component), `components/PriceRangeSlider.test.tsx` **Structure Stub (ARIA injected onto each thumb):** ```
          Price range

          Price: ${minValue}–${maxValue}

          (visible readout; SR uses aria-valuetext)
          ``` **ARIA:** two `role="slider"` (4.1.2); `aria-label` per thumb (4.1.2); `aria-valuemin/max/now` with cross-thumb clamping (4.1.2); `aria-valuetext` formatted with "$" updated each step (4.1.2). Group named by `` (1.3.1). **Keyboard (APG Multi-Thumb Slider):** Tab to each thumb (two tab stops); ←/↓ decrease, →/↑ increase by step; PageDown/PageUp larger step; Home/End to that thumb's bound; min can't exceed max and vice-versa. **Focus management:** each thumb is an independent tab stop; visible focus ring on thumbs (override third-party CSS). **State:** value via `aria-valuenow` + `aria-valuetext`; visible readout updates; dynamic `aria-valuemin/max` reflect the clamp. **Tests:** **(a)** both thumbs have `role="slider"` and all four value attrs; **(b)** arrow/Home/End/Page keys move values correctly; **(c)** clamping prevents crossing and updates the neighbor's `aria-valuemin/max`; **(d)** `aria-valuetext` announces "$" value each step (AT, all three SRs); **(e)** thumb hit area ≥24×24 with visible focus; **(f)** operable at 400% zoom. **WCAG:** 4.1.2, 2.1.1, 1.3.1, 1.4.13, 2.5.8. **a11y-critic checkpoint:** NOT a bare `` and NOT relying on the library's (absent) ARIA; two `role="slider"` with per-thumb labels; `aria-valuetext` formatted; cross-thumb clamp reflected in min/max; keyboard model complete; focus visible. ### Task 9: Pagination navigation 🔍 **Review checkpoint 9** (may share with Task 7) **Files:** `components/Pagination.tsx`, `components/Pagination.test.tsx` **Structure Stub:** ```
          ‹ Previous page {n} … Next ›
          ``` On page change (SPA): move focus to results region; announce range via Task 7 region. **ARIA:** `
          ` (1.3.1, 2.4.1); `aria-current="page"` on current (4.1.2); descriptive link names (2.4.4); glyphs `aria-hidden`; prev disabled at page 1 via `aria-disabled` (4.1.2). **Keyboard:** Tab to links; Enter activates. **Focus management:** after navigation, focus lands in results region (not lost to body); `tabindex="-1"` on the results container if used as the focus target. **Tests:** named nav landmark; exactly one `aria-current="page"`; descriptive names; glyphs hidden; focus restored after page change; range announced. **WCAG:** 1.3.1, 2.4.1, 2.4.3, 2.4.4, 4.1.2, 4.1.3. **a11y-critic checkpoint:** named `
          `; single `aria-current`; focus restoration after SPA page change; prev/next not bare glyphs. --- ## a11y-Critic Review Checkpoints | Checkpoint | After Task | Focus | |-----------|-----------|-------| | 🔍 1 | Task 1 | `
            `/`
          • ` (not div-grid); H3 product-name link; no redundant adjacent same-destination links; image-alt strategy | | 🔍 2 | Task 2 | Star pattern exact (aria-hidden glyphs + sr-only text), not bare span aria-label; raw glyphs silenced | | 🔍 3 | Task 3 | Sale price: aria-hidden struck original + "was/now" sr-only; spoken once; not `` alone; sale = text | | 🔍 4 | Task 4 | Availability text not color-only; out-of-stock disables button + exposes state | | 🔍 5 | Task 5 | Native ``, native checkboxes, `

_benchmark.method: plan 006 Phase D Claude lane: Agent(subagent_type=a11y-planner, model=opus), one background subagent per fixture; verbatim PLANNER_PROMPT_PREFIX task; agent wrote plan via Write tool; orchestrator wrapped to response JSON
